summary: Large unmerged batch PR that converted many decomp stubs and near-matches across fighter, stage, item, game-mode, interface, and library code. The PR body listed 33 direct 100% matches, 15 near-perfect matches, 5 partial matches, and 96 incidental improvements; the decomp.dev bot reported a much larger aggregate effect of +2.10% matched code, +81,408 matched bytes, +128,704 linked bytes, and 341 new matches. The diff also toggled several translation units from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, including ftcoll.c, ft_0892.c, ftCo_Guard.c, ftCo_Attack100.c, ftYs_SpecialS.c, ftCh_Init.c, and grcorneria.c. The PR was closed without merge in the provided slice, with no human review comments explaining why.
what_changed: Replaced many `/// #function` placeholders with C implementations across multiple systems, including examples shown for ftCo_800D7268, fn_800D7830, fn_800D7BDC, ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, ftCh_Init_80155FCC, fn_80157080, ftCh_Damage2_Phys, ftCh_BackCrush_Anim, ftCh_BackDisappear_Anim, ftCh_Cancel_Anim, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC, and static fn_800F1CA0.

Updated configure.py to mark several translation units as Matching instead of NonMatching: melee/ft/ftcoll.c, melee/ft/ft_0892.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c, melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS.c, melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c, and melee/gr/grcorneria.c.

Added or corrected prototypes and externs in headers, such as `s32 ftCo_800D7268(void*)`, `void fn_800D7938(Fighter_GObj*)`, `s32 ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8(HSD_GObj*)`, `ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0`, `ftCh_Init_8015A184`, and Crazy Hand float externs `ftCh_Init_804DA070` and `ftCh_Init_804DA074`.

Used localized matching aids in shown code, including `PAD_STACK(8)` in ftCo_Guard animation functions, `M2C_FIELD` offset accesses in ftCo_Attack100 and Crazy Hand code, explicit static forward declarations, and float-literal style such as `0.0F`/`1.0F` where needed.

Added CLAUDE.md containing repository guidance for build commands, objdiff/decomp.me workflow, module prefixes, struct offset-field conventions, literal formatting, NULL checks, and other decomp coding conventions.
The bot report noted one unmatched-function regression in `main/melee/it/it_2725` for `it_8027990C` from 98.42% to 82.37%, so the batch was not uniformly positive in matching metrics.
smart_moves: The PR leveraged small local fixes to unlock broad unit-level matching: decomp.dev reported 341 new matches and the PR body explicitly called out 96 incidental improvements from related/shared code changes.
Promoting several units to Matching in configure.py after implementation work made the build configuration reflect actual matching status rather than leaving newly matched files in NonMatching mode.
Header cleanup converted some unknown prototypes to typed signatures, improving cross-file call matching and making call sites less dependent on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

The ftCo_Guard changes demonstrate that tiny codegen-sensitive adjustments, such as adding an expected call inside a static inline and using `PAD_STACK(8)`, can make multiple guard IASA/animation-related functions match incidentally.

The PR kept many newly matched functions close to existing naming/address conventions rather than inventing speculative semantic names, which is appropriate when evidence is still mostly address-based.
decomp_lessons: Batch matching can produce very large progress, but the author explicitly asked whether the batch was too many matches at once; future PRs should consider splitting by subsystem or matching unit to reduce review risk.
When a function is still semantically unclear, preserving address-based names like `fn_800D7830` or `ftCh_Init_80155FCC` is safer than over-naming without evidence.
Shared inlines, callbacks, and prototypes can affect many functions at once; measure incidental improvements and regressions with bot reports before assuming a local change only affects one function.
Small stack-shape controls such as `PAD_STACK(8)` are valid matching tactics when used narrowly and with diff evidence.

Offset-based field access through `M2C_FIELD` can be useful for matching when struct fields are not yet known, but it should be treated as a temporary decomp aid and later replaced with named fields when layouts are understood.
After a translation unit reaches matching status, update configure.py in the same PR so linked/matched-code metrics reflect the decomp progress.
Bot metrics should be checked for regressions in unmatched functions; this PR’s `it_8027990C` regression shows that a large net win can still hide a local backslide.
naming_conventions: Function names retain module prefixes and address suffixes when semantics are unknown, e.g. `ftCo_800D7268`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC`, `ftCh_Init_80155FCC`, `grZebes_801D84A4`, `lbBgFlash_800205F0`.

Fighter-specific filenames and prefixes follow existing two-letter character abbreviations: Crazy Hand uses `ftCh_`, Kirby uses `ftKb_`, Popo uses `ftPp_`, Yoshi uses `ftYs_`, and Common fighter code uses `ftCo_`.
Header declarations in the diff use address comments such as `/* 0D7268 */`, `/* 0F1CD8 */`, and `/* 121DA0 */` to preserve address indexing.

CLAUDE.md documents project conventions: function prefix should match filename/module, struct fields use hex offset prefixes such as `x0_` and `x10_`, literals use uppercase suffixes like `1.23F`, and explicit NULL checks are preferred.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marking complete units as `Object(Matching, ...)` in configure.py is the final step after enough functions in that object have been matched.
Use `PAD_STACK(8)` to reproduce stack frame shape when the generated code otherwise differs, as shown in `ftCo_Guard_Anim` and `ftCo_GuardOff_Anim`.

Use `M2C_FIELD` for unresolved struct offsets when exact layout/names are not yet available, as shown with Fighter offsets `0x10`, `0x894`, `0x89C`, `0x21DC`, and Crazy Hand offsets around `0x2344`/`0x234C`/`0x2358`.

Static forward declarations can help match intra-file call ordering and typing, e.g. `static void fn_800D80F4(Fighter_GObj*)`, `static void ftCh_Init_80156310(HSD_GObj*)`, and `static bool fn_800F1CA0(HSD_GObj*)`.
Explicitly typed callback/function-pointer assignments were used in matched code, such as assigning `fp->accessory4_cb`, `fp->take_dmg_cb`, and a callback at Fighter offset `0x21DC`.
Function bodies often used exact motion-state constants and float values to reproduce assembly, e.g. `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls with constants like `0x158`, `0x0C4C5280`, `0.0F`, and `1.0F`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased to 49.64% (+2.10%, +81,408 bytes) and linked code increased to 31.91% (+3.32%, +128,704 bytes).
The bot reported 341 new matches, 22 improvements in unmatched functions, and 1 regression in unmatched functions.
The PR was closed without merge, but the provided slice does not include a stated reason for closing.
